bearerAuth.description: Secret API key issued in the dashboard, sent as a Bearer token.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was created.

share.description: URL to a hosted page for this job. Shows a state-aware page (queued, running, failed) while the job is pre-terminal and the full HTML summary once it completes. The page does not auto-refresh today; reload to see the latest state. Returns `404` after retention expires.

manifest.description: URL to the manifest JSON for the latest attempt. Only present once the job reaches a terminal status. Returns `404` after retention expires. May change between responses if the job is retried.

post.description:
Submits a new SimC sim to the queue. Returns immediately with a job ID; the sim runs asynchronously. Use the ID to poll status, fetch the final result, and download any artifacts.

The job's credit budget (`runtime.maxCredits`, plus any priority fee) is held from your balance at submission and reconciled against actual usage on completion. See "Credits → Job budgets" in the documentation for details.

schema.description: Optional client-supplied key (≤255 printable-ASCII chars) for safe retries. Reusing the same key with the same payload returns the original job; reusing it with a different payload returns 409. `metadata` is excluded from the payload comparison, so you can change it across retries. Keys are scoped to your API key and this endpoint, and do not expire.

parameters.description: Optional client-supplied key (≤255 printable-ASCII chars) for safe retries. Reusing the same key with the same payload returns the original job; reusing it with a different payload returns 409. `metadata` is excluded from the payload comparison, so you can change it across retries. Keys are scoped to your API key and this endpoint, and do not expire.
parameters.name: idempotency-key

gitBranch.description: Optional SimC git branch to override the channel's default.

text.description: SimC profile text to execute (the content you would normally paste into a local simc run). Maximum 2 MB (UTF-8 encoded).

multiStage.description: Opt-in to multistage execution with automatic culling between stages. When false or omitted, the sim runs in a single pass.

maxCredits.description: Credit budget for this job. The full budget (plus any priority fee) is held from your balance at submission and the unused portion is refunded when the job settles; a job whose usage reaches the budget is stopped with `errorCode` `max_credits_reached` and billed the budget. Defaults to 9600 credits when omitted, which covers most jobs. To size from expected sim time, multiply seconds by `plan.creditsPerSecond` and add headroom. The budget applied is returned in the response as `runtime.ceiling.maxCredits`; the maximum is `plan.maxCreditsPerJob` on `GET /v1/simc/usage`.

maxRuntimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `maxCredits`. Interpreted as a credit budget of this many seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second (`maxCredits = maxRuntimeSeconds × 32`). Do not send both fields.

maxQueueSeconds.description: Maximum time this job may wait in the queue before being auto-cancelled, in seconds. Defaults to 1800 seconds when omitted. The ceiling applied is returned in the response as `runtime.ceiling.queueSeconds`.

runtime.description: Execution options for this job. Multistage execution is skipped if your input contains unsupported patterns or directives.

priority.description: Queue priority for this job. Allowed values: `background`, `standard`, `high`. Higher priority runs first; same-priority jobs run in submission order. `high` adds a flat per-job fee only when it would buy a faster start. The response reports the fee charged in `runtime.priorityFeeCredits`.

metadata.description: Opaque metadata echoed back unchanged for your own correlation or labels (for example `profileSource`, `characterRef`). Not used for scheduling or sim execution.

bnetClientId.description: Battle.net API client ID. Must be provided together with `bnetClientSecret`. Lets SimC perform armory/guild imports and resolve item data in your profile that is missing from the current build. Used only for this job and never persisted.

bnetClientSecret.description: Battle.net API client secret. Must be provided together with `bnetClientId`. Lets SimC perform armory/guild imports and resolve item data in your profile that is missing from the current build. Used only for this job and never persisted.

credentials.description: Optional per-job credentials. These values are used only for this job and are never persisted.

events.description: Webhook event types to subscribe to for this job.

webhook.description: Optional webhook subscription settings for this job. Delivery URL and signing secret come from your webhook configuration.

html.description: Whether to generate an HTML report. Defaults to `false`.

csv.description: Whether to generate a CSV result table (`result.csv`): the baseline plus one row per profileset, columns `name,dps_mean,dps_min,dps_max,dps_std_dev,dps_mean_std_dev`. Defaults to `false`.

version.description: JSON report schema version. `2` = stable v2.0.0, `3` = v3.0.0-alpha1 (experimental). Defaults to `2`.

json.description: JSON report options. A JSON report is always generated; use this to select the schema version.

artifacts.description: Controls which output formats are produced. Omit for the default (JSON v2 only, no HTML or CSV). Logs and input artifacts are always produced regardless. For multistage runs, HTML is generated only for the final stage; the CSV table covers every profileset across all stages.

200.description: Sim accepted and queued. The returned job ID is your handle for polling, fetching the result, and downloading artifacts.

schema.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

X-Active-Jobs.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

schema.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

X-Max-Active-Jobs.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

schema.description: Your account ceiling for job submissions per minute.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Your account ceiling for job submissions per minute.

schema.description: Submissions you can still make in the current minute.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Submissions you can still make in the current minute.

schema.description: Unix epoch seconds when your remaining submissions refill.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix epoch seconds when your remaining submissions refill.

schema.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

schema.description: Present and equal to `true` only when the response is a replay of an earlier submission with the same `idempotency-key`. Absent on fresh submissions, including the first request that consumed the key.

X-Idempotent-Replay.description: Present and equal to `true` only when the response is a replay of an earlier submission with the same `idempotency-key`. Absent on fresh submissions, including the first request that consumed the key.

maxCredits.description: The credit budget that applied to this run: the amount held at submission (plus any priority fee), and the usage at which the job is stopped with `errorCode` `max_credits_reached`. For jobs that predate credit budgets, this is derived from the recorded runtime cap at the basis rate of 32 credits per second. View the maximum at `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.maxCreditsPerJob`.

runtimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `maxCredits`. The budget expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

queueSeconds.description: The queue timeout ceiling that applied to this run, in seconds. Jobs that wait in the queue longer than this are auto-cancelled with `errorCode` `queue_timeout`. View the maximum at `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.maxQueueSeconds`.

priorityFeeCredits.description: Priority fee for this job, included in the credit reservation. `0` when no fee applied.

kind.description: Machine-readable warning category. `iterations_clamped` for `iterations=` values above the platform safety cap; `target_error_floored` for `target_error=` values below the platform safety floor.

requested.description: The value as written in your input.

applied.description: The clamped or floored value the sim runs with.

warnings.description: Warnings about your input. Omitted when there are none. Covers `iterations=` values above the platform safety cap and `target_error=` values below the platform safety floor. The job still runs at the clamped or floored value.

field.description: The deprecated request field, for example `runtime.maxRuntimeSeconds`.

message.description: How the value was interpreted and which field replaces it.

deprecations.description: Notices about deprecated request fields your submission used. Omitted when there are none. The job is unaffected; each notice explains the replacement field.

400.description: Your request was missing required input or failed validation. See `error` for the specific issue.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

402.description: The request was rejected because your account has insufficient credits or is inactive. See `code` and `meta` for details. For credit shortfalls, `meta.maxAffordableCredits` indicates the largest `runtime.maxCredits` your current balance can cover.

ceilingMaxCredits.description: The credit budget the rejected submission would have run under.

maxAffordableCredits.description: Largest `runtime.maxCredits` your current balance covers (after any priority fee).

ceilingRuntimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: `ceilingMaxCredits` expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

maxAffordableRuntimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: `maxAffordableCredits` expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

docsUrl.description: Link to the relevant credits/billing docs for this rejection.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

409.description: The supplied `idempotency-key` was previously used with a different request payload. `meta.originalJobId` is the id of the job that originally consumed the key.

originalJobId.description: ID of the job that originally consumed this idempotency key.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

413.description: Your request body exceeds the 3 MB maximum. A profile over 2 MB returns code `profile_too_large` instead.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

422.description: Your input was rejected. `code` is `input_sanitized_rejected` when your profile contains directives Simmit does not allow (`meta.docsUrl` links to the directives reference), or `too_many_variants` when it expands to more simulated variants than your account allows (`meta.upgradeUrl` links to your account page, where a higher limit can be requested). `meta.message` explains the rejection.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of why the input was rejected.

docsUrl.description: Link to the SimC directives reference.

blocked.description: A sample of rejected lines. The full count may exceed the sample — see `blockedCount` and `blockedTruncated`.

blockedTruncated.description: `true` when more lines were rejected than included in `blocked`.

totalVariants.description: Simulated variants your input expands to, counting `profileset`, `copy`, and `set` directives.

maxVariants.description: Maximum simulated variants per sim for your account.

upgradeUrl.description: Link to your account page, where a higher variant limit can be requested.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

429.description: Your account has exceeded its request rate or in-flight job limit. See `code` and `meta` for the specific cause. `Retry-After` carries the next-attempt time.

schema.description: Seconds to wait before retrying.
Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

activeJobs.description: Jobs your account had in flight when this request was rejected.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

503.description: Your sim was not accepted right now. `code` indicates the cause: `queue_unavailable` when the queue is full or paused, `queue_health_unknown` when queue health cannot be confirmed, `secret_store_unavailable` when per-job credentials supplied with the request could not be stored, or `api_maintenance` during planned maintenance. `Retry-After` carries the next-attempt time.

schema.description: Seconds to wait before retrying.

parameters.description: Maximum number of jobs to return. Defaults to 25, max 100.

200.description: Available SimC builds, grouped by channel.

gitBranch.description: Git branch this build was produced from.

repo.description: Source repository for this SimC build.

commit.description: Git commit hash of the SimC source used for this build.

simcVersion.description: SimC program version for this build (e.g. `1205-01`). Null when not recorded.

hotfixDate.description: Hotfix date of the live game data (`YYYY-MM-DD`).
Hotfix date of the PTR game data (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

ptr.description: PTR game version and hotfix date, or null if this build has no PTR data.

gameData.description: The live and PTR WoW game versions this build simulates against. Null when not recorded.

status.description: Computed from gate checks: pass = all gated checks green, partial = gate checks green but other failures exist, fail = gate checks failed (fallback build), unknown = CI data unavailable.

failures.description: Names of check runs with non-success conclusions (failure, cancelled, timed_out, action_required). Full GitHub job names as-is.

gatedOn.description: Glob patterns describing which checks were used as the build gate.

retrievedAt.description: When CI state was fetched from GitHub.

ci.description: Upstream CI status for this commit, with per-check details. Null when CI data is unavailable.

manifest.description: Profile filenames this build accepts in submitted profile text (e.g. `MID1_Mage_Arcane.simc`). Each file is verified at build time to parse and sim cleanly for one iteration. Reference one in your profile text on its own line or as `input=<filename>`. Sorted lexicographically.

profiles.description: Bundled profiles available for this build. See https://docs.simmit.com/api/bundled-profiles for more.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

get.description: Returns a SimC build by ID, including older builds no longer current on any channel. `GET /v1/simc/builds` lists only the current build per channel. Returns `404` once the build has been removed from retention.

schema.description: Build ID, as returned in `build.id` on any job response.

get.description: Returns the full record for a SimC job, including timestamps, runtime details, metadata, and build info. For lightweight polling while a sim runs, use `GET /v1/simc/jobs/{id}/status` instead.

schema.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

X-Active-Jobs.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

schema.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

X-Max-Active-Jobs.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

schema.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

priority.description: Effective priority the job is running at.

startedAt.description: When this job began executing. Null when the job has not yet started.

cancelRequestedAt.description: When a cancel was requested. Null when no cancel has been requested.

statusReason.description: Human-readable explanation for the terminal status, paired with `errorCode`.

retentionDays.description: Number of days artifacts are retained after `completedAt`. After this window, artifact endpoints return `410`.

metadata.description: Opaque metadata echoed back exactly as submitted (for example `profileSource`, `characterRef`). Not used for scheduling or sim execution.

simDurationMs.description: SimC process wall-clock time in milliseconds. Excludes setup and teardown time. Null if the job has not completed or the sim did not start.

totalDurationMs.description: Total wall-clock time from job start to completion (milliseconds). Includes sim execution, artifact generation, and post-processing. Null when the job has not yet run.

creditsConsumed.description: Credits charged for this job, including any priority fee. `0` for terminal jobs with no billable work. Null until the job reaches a terminal state.

priorityFeeCredits.description: Priority fee for this job, included in `creditsConsumed` when the job is billed. `0` when no fee applied. View the current fee from `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.priorityFeeCredits.high`.

creditsPerSecond.description: Metering rate applied to this run, in credits per second of SimC runtime: `creditsConsumed` is this rate times `simDurationMs` rounded up to whole seconds, plus `priorityFeeCredits`. Null until the job is assigned to a machine.

vcpus.description: vCPU count of the machine this run executed on. Determines how fast a sim completes; billing uses `creditsPerSecond`. Null before execution.

maxCredits.description: The credit budget that applied to this run: the amount held at submission (plus any priority fee), and the usage at which the job is stopped with `errorCode` `max_credits_reached`. For jobs that predate credit budgets, this is derived from the recorded runtime cap at the basis rate of 32 credits per second. View the maximum at `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.maxCreditsPerJob`.

runtimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `maxCredits`. The budget expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

queueSeconds.description: The queue timeout ceiling that applied to this run, in seconds. Jobs that wait in the queue longer than this are auto-cancelled with `errorCode` `queue_timeout`. View the maximum at `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.maxQueueSeconds`.

retried.description: True if this job was retried automatically because of a platform error. The response reflects the latest retry attempt.

id.description: Build ID. `GET /v1/simc/builds/{id}` returns the full build record.

commit.description: Git commit hash of the SimC source used for this build.

status.description: Computed CI status for the build commit. See GET /v1/simc/builds for full check details.

failed.description: Number of CI checks with non-success conclusions (failure, cancelled, timed_out, action_required). See GET /v1/simc/builds for check names.

ci.description: Compact upstream CI summary (status and failure count) for this commit. The full per-check breakdown is on `GET /v1/simc/builds/{id}`. Null when CI data is unavailable.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.
Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

get.description: Returns the full result payload for a finished SimC job — summary metrics, runtime info, and artifact references. Available once the job reaches a terminal status (`completed`, `failed`, `cancelled`, or `timed_out`).

200.description: Full result payload for a finished job — summary metrics, runtime info, and artifact references.

priority.description: Effective priority the job is running at.

startedAt.description: When this job began executing. Null when the job has not yet started.

cancelRequestedAt.description: When a cancel was requested. Null when no cancel has been requested.

statusReason.description: Human-readable explanation for the terminal status, paired with `errorCode`.

retentionDays.description: Number of days artifacts are retained after `completedAt`. After this window, artifact endpoints return `410`.

metadata.description: Opaque metadata echoed back exactly as submitted (for example `profileSource`, `characterRef`). Not used for scheduling or sim execution.

simDurationMs.description: SimC process wall-clock time in milliseconds. Excludes setup and teardown time. Null if the job has not completed or the sim did not start.

totalDurationMs.description: Total wall-clock time from job start to completion (milliseconds). Includes sim execution, artifact generation, and post-processing. Null when the job has not yet run.

creditsConsumed.description: Credits charged for this job, including any priority fee. `0` for terminal jobs with no billable work. Null until the job reaches a terminal state.

priorityFeeCredits.description: Priority fee for this job, included in `creditsConsumed` when the job is billed. `0` when no fee applied. View the current fee from `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.priorityFeeCredits.high`.

creditsPerSecond.description: Metering rate applied to this run, in credits per second of SimC runtime: `creditsConsumed` is this rate times `simDurationMs` rounded up to whole seconds, plus `priorityFeeCredits`. Null until the job is assigned to a machine.

vcpus.description: vCPU count of the machine this run executed on. Determines how fast a sim completes; billing uses `creditsPerSecond`. Null before execution.

maxCredits.description: The credit budget that applied to this run: the amount held at submission (plus any priority fee), and the usage at which the job is stopped with `errorCode` `max_credits_reached`. For jobs that predate credit budgets, this is derived from the recorded runtime cap at the basis rate of 32 credits per second. View the maximum at `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.maxCreditsPerJob`.

runtimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `maxCredits`. The budget expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

queueSeconds.description: The queue timeout ceiling that applied to this run, in seconds. Jobs that wait in the queue longer than this are auto-cancelled with `errorCode` `queue_timeout`. View the maximum at `GET /v1/simc/usage` under `plan.maxQueueSeconds`.

retried.description: True if this job was retried automatically because of a platform error. The response reflects the latest retry attempt.

id.description: Build ID. `GET /v1/simc/builds/{id}` returns the full build record.

commit.description: Git commit hash of the SimC source used for this build.

status.description: Computed CI status for the build commit. See GET /v1/simc/builds for full check details.

failed.description: Number of CI checks with non-success conclusions (failure, cancelled, timed_out, action_required). See GET /v1/simc/builds for check names.

ci.description: Compact upstream CI summary (status and failure count) for this commit. The full per-check breakdown is on `GET /v1/simc/builds/{id}`. Null when CI data is unavailable.

metric.description: The metric that `mean` and `mean_error` are reported in and that profilesets are ranked by. `dps` is the main actor's own damage (the default). `raid_dps` is the whole group's combined damage, returned when your input sets `profileset_metric=raid_dps`; in that case `mainActor.mean` is the group total.

name.description: Actor name as defined in your profile.

mean_error.description: The ± DPS error on `mean` — the half-width of its 95% confidence interval, equal to `mean_stddev` × 1.96. This is the same error SimC reports.

mean_stddev.description: Standard error of the mean DPS (the standard deviation of the mean estimate).

count.description: Total number of profilesets in the sim result. When this exceeds the length of `results`, the array was truncated to the top 200 entries by DPS. The full set is available in the JSON artifact.

name.description: Profileset name as defined in your input.

mean_error.description: The ± DPS error on `mean` — the half-width of its 95% confidence interval, equal to `mean_stddev` × 1.96. This is the same error SimC reports.

mean_stddev.description: Standard error of the mean DPS (the standard deviation of the mean estimate).

stage.description: For multistage runs, the highest stage this profileset reached — it was culled after this stage, or it survived to the final stage. Earlier stages run at a coarser `target_error`, so a result from an earlier stage carries a larger `mean_error` than a final-stage result. `1` for single-pass runs.

results.description: Profileset sweep entries, ranked by DPS (highest first). Truncated to the top 200 entries — see `count` and the JSON artifact for the full set.

profilesets.description: Profileset sweep results scoped to this actor (mirrors SimC's `sim.profilesets` block shape). Omitted when no profileset results are available — either the input declared no profilesets, or none were produced.

mainActor.description: Result for the headline actor (selected by SimC's `profileset_main_actor_index` directive, default `0`). When profileset results are available, this object also carries a `profilesets` block (`{ count, results }`) for the sweep over this actor; the block is omitted when no results are available (the input declared no profilesets, or none were produced). For multi-player sims using `copy=` or `set=`, per-player results for the remaining actors are in the JSON artifact — that multi-player case is separate from the profileset sweep.

reason.description: Why multistage execution did or did not run.

profilesets.description: Number of profilesets executed in this stage.

culled.description: Number of profilesets eliminated at the end of this stage based on ranking. These do not advance to the next stage. The last executed stage always reports `0` (no culling occurs after the last stage).

stages.description: Per-stage funnel for multistage execution, in execution order. Empty for single-pass runs.

url.description: Stable public URL for downloading this artifact. Valid for the full retention window of the job.

kind.description: Machine-readable artifact category. `html_report`: human-readable HTML report. `json_report`: machine-readable JSON results. `csv_report`: per-profileset DPS table (`result.csv`, columns `name,dps_mean,dps_min,dps_max,dps_std_dev,dps_mean_std_dev`); a single job-level artifact (`stage` null) covering every profileset across all stages. `input`: the exact SimC input your job ran. `stdout_log` / `stderr_log`: raw SimC logs. Under multistage execution html/json artifacts are identified by `kind` together with `stage` (the same `kind` appears once per stage); `stage` is null for single-run jobs and for `csv_report`.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the artifact content, drawn from a fixed set: `application/json`, `text/html`, `text/csv`, or `text/plain`.

stage.description: Stage number for multistage execution artifacts (1-indexed). Null for single-run jobs or artifacts not associated with a specific multistage stage.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

422.description: Result unavailable. The job is terminal but no result payload was produced.

get.summary: Get a SimC job’s submitted profile
get.description: Returns the SimC profile text you submitted with the job. Available at any point in the job’s lifecycle, including while the job is queued or running.

200.description: The SimC profile text you submitted with the job.

schema.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

X-Active-Jobs.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

schema.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

X-Max-Active-Jobs.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

schema.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

text.description: The exact SimC profile text you submitted with this job. `null` if no profile text is stored for the job.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.
Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

get.description: Returns the current status, queue position, and progress for a SimC job. Designed for polling while a sim runs. Use `include` to add optional fields like `logEntries`.

schema.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include. Supported: `logEntries` (structured log entries from stdout and stderr for running jobs).

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include. Supported: `logEntries` (structured log entries from stdout and stderr for running jobs).
parameters.name: include

200.description: Current status, queue position, and progress for the job.

schema.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

X-Active-Jobs.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

schema.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

X-Max-Active-Jobs.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

schema.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

status.description: Current job status. Terminal values: completed, failed, cancelled, timed_out.

errorCode.description: Machine-readable error code for the terminal state. Null when the job is not terminal or completed successfully.

statusReason.description: Human-readable explanation paired with `errorCode`. Null when the job is not terminal or no additional detail is available.

simcExitCode.description: SimC process exit code. Null when the job is not terminal.

queuedAt.description: When this job entered the queue. For a retried job, this reflects the current attempt, not the original submission.

estimatedStartSeconds.description: Estimated seconds until this job begins running. Updated on each poll; not a guarantee. Null when no estimate is available.

estimatedStartUpdatedAt.description: When the underlying wait-time estimate was last refreshed. Null when no estimate is available.

percent.description: Estimated completion percentage (0–100). Null when not yet running.

total.description: Total number of stages in this run.

label.description: Human-readable name of the current stage (for example "initial", "intermediate", "final").

percent.description: Estimated completion percentage (0–100) within the current stage.

stage.description: Stage progress detail while the job is running. Null before the job starts running and once it reaches a terminal state. Single-pass jobs report `{ current: 1, total: 1, label: "initial" }` with `percent` populated while running.

source.description: Which stream the line came from. `stderr` lines are diagnostic output — SimC warnings, errors, and notes. `stdout` lines are sim output, progress markers, and results.

ts.description: Epoch milliseconds (UTC) when this line was captured.

logEntries.description: Recent log lines from SimC stdout and stderr while the job is running. Each entry is tagged with its stream (`source`) and capture time (`ts`). Full logs are available as downloadable artifacts once the job completes. Null when not running or not requested via `include=logEntries`.

startedAt.description: When this job began executing. Null when the job has not yet started.

retried.description: True if this job was retried automatically because of a platform error. The response reflects the latest retry attempt.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.
Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

get.description: Returns a stable public URL for a SimC job artifact (HTML report, JSON report, raw log, etc.), valid for the artifact's full retention window. Use this when you need to control the final fetch URL yourself — for example, browser flows that need explicit cross-origin handling.

200.description: A stable public URL for downloading the artifact. Valid for the artifact's full retention window.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.
Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

410.description: The artifact has expired and is no longer available.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

post.description: Cancels one of your SimC jobs. Jobs that have not started running end immediately. Jobs already in flight are signaled to stop; poll `GET /v1/simc/jobs/{id}/status` to observe the transition to `cancelled`. Repeat calls return the same response.

200.description: Cancel accepted. `status` is `cancelled` when the job ended before running, or `cancel_requested` when the job was already running and has been signaled to stop.

status.description: The job ended before it started running and is fully cancelled.
The job was already running and has been signaled to stop. Poll `GET /v1/simc/jobs/{id}` to observe the transition to `cancelled`.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.
Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

409.description: Job has already reached a terminal status.

get.description: Returns your usage for the current period along with a live snapshot of queued and in-flight jobs. For your credit balance, see `GET /v1/simc/credits`.

200.description: Your usage summary, including both period-based metrics and live snapshot counters.

schema.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

X-Active-Jobs.description: Your in-flight job count at the time of this response.

schema.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

X-Max-Active-Jobs.description: Your account ceiling for in-flight jobs.

schema.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

start.description: UTC start of the usage period.

end.description: UTC end of the usage period.

simsRun.description: Completed sims in the current period.

avgRuntimeSeconds.description: Average runtime in seconds for completed sims in the current period.

readsUsed.description: API read requests consumed in the current period.

readsLimit.description: Maximum read requests allowed in the current period. Null means no cap.

readsCapResetAt.description: When the current reads cap resets.

activeJobs.description: Number of jobs currently in flight. The ceiling is `plan.maxActiveJobs`.

runningJobs.description: Number of jobs currently running or starting.

maxActiveJobs.description: Maximum jobs your account can have in flight.

maxCreditsPerJob.description: Largest credit budget (`runtime.maxCredits`) a job submission may request.

defaultCreditsPerJob.description: Credit budget applied when a submission omits `runtime.maxCredits`.

maxRuntimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `maxCreditsPerJob`. The same ceiling expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

defaultRuntimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `defaultCreditsPerJob`. The same default expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

maxQueueSeconds.description: Maximum time in seconds a job may wait in the queue before being cancelled.

defaultQueueSeconds.description: Default queue wait per job in seconds.

creditsPerSecond.description: Metering rate for your jobs, in credits per second of SimC runtime. Usage accrues at this rate while a sim runs, so a budget of `runtime.maxCredits` buys about `maxCredits / creditsPerSecond` seconds. The rate applied to a finished run is reported on the job as `runtime.creditsPerSecond`.

vcpuPerJob.description: vCPUs allocated to each job. Determines how fast a sim completes; billing uses `creditsPerSecond`.

maxSimcVariants.description: Maximum simc variants (profilesets, copies, sets) allowed per job. Null means no cap.

pool.description: Compute pool your jobs run on. `standard` runs on on-demand capacity, so a job may wait for a machine to boot. `warm` keeps an always-on floor of capacity. `dedicated` is capacity reserved for your account on a custom plan.

priorityFeeCredits.description: Per-job priority fee in credits, by priority. Added to the base compute cost for jobs submitted at `high` only when it would buy a faster start.

plan.description: The terms your account runs under: metering rate, runtime and queue ceilings, concurrency and variant caps, compute pool, and priority fees.

maxActiveJobs.description: Maximum jobs your account can have in flight.

maxCreditsPerJob.description: Largest credit budget (`runtime.maxCredits`) a job submission may request.

defaultCreditsPerJob.description: Credit budget applied when a submission omits `runtime.maxCredits`.

maxRuntimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `maxCreditsPerJob`. The same ceiling expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

defaultRuntimeSeconds.description: Deprecated: use `defaultCreditsPerJob`. The same default expressed in seconds at the basis rate of 32 credits per second.

maxQueueSeconds.description: Maximum time in seconds a job may wait in the queue before being cancelled.

defaultQueueSeconds.description: Default queue wait per job in seconds.

creditsPerSecond.description: Metering rate for your jobs, in credits per second of SimC runtime. Usage accrues at this rate while a sim runs, so a budget of `runtime.maxCredits` buys about `maxCredits / creditsPerSecond` seconds. The rate applied to a finished run is reported on the job as `runtime.creditsPerSecond`.

vcpuPerJob.description: vCPUs allocated to each job. Determines how fast a sim completes; billing uses `creditsPerSecond`.

maxSimcVariants.description: Maximum simc variants (profilesets, copies, sets) allowed per job. Null means no cap.

pool.description: Compute pool your jobs run on. `standard` runs on on-demand capacity, so a job may wait for a machine to boot. `warm` keeps an always-on floor of capacity. `dedicated` is capacity reserved for your account on a custom plan.

priorityFeeCredits.description: Per-job priority fee in credits, by priority. Added to the base compute cost for jobs submitted at `high` only when it would buy a faster start.

limits.description: Deprecated alias of `plan` with identical contents.

schema.description: Your usage: period metrics, live snapshot counters, and your plan terms. For your credit balance, see `GET /v1/simc/credits`.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

get.summary: Get your credit balance and active grants
get.description:
Returns your credit balance, split between purchased credits and any active grants. Total available credits equal `purchased + sum(grants[].remaining)`. `reserved` reports credits currently held against in-flight jobs and is already excluded from both `purchased` and `grants[].remaining`.

Consumption order: grants are spent before purchased credits, with the earliest-expiring grant spent first. Grants without an expiry are spent last.

For transaction history and lifetime totals, see the dashboard.

200.description: Your credit balance and active grants.

schema.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

X-Request-Id.description: Correlation id for this request, present on every response. The same value is echoed in the `requestId` field of error bodies.

purchased.description: Total purchased credit balance. Purchased credits do not expire.

reserved.description: Credits currently held against your in-flight jobs. Refunded on settlement (any unused amount returns to `purchased` and `grants[].remaining`). Excluded from `purchased` and `grants[].remaining`.

reason.description: The reason this grant was issued.

remaining.description: Credits currently available in this grant. Excludes any credits already committed to in-flight jobs.

original.description: Credits originally issued in this grant.

expiresAt.description: When this grant expires. `null` means the grant has no expiry. After expiry, any remaining credits in the grant are forfeited and the grant no longer appears in this response.

items.description: A single active grant. Each grant is a separate pool of credits with its own balance and optional expiry.

grants.description: Your active grants, ordered by expiry (soonest first; grants without expiry last). Empty when no grants are active.

schema.description: Your credit balance. Total available credits equal `purchased + sum(grants[].remaining)`.

401.description: Your API token is missing, invalid, or not authorized for this request. See `code` for the specific reason.

requestId.description: Correlation id for this request, also returned in the `X-Request-Id` response header.

post.description: Sent when a subscribed job reaches a terminal state (completed, failed, cancelled, or timed_out). Configure your endpoint URL and signing secret in the dashboard, then subscribe individual jobs by including webhook.events in the POST /simc/jobs request body. Every delivery is signed — verify X-Simmit-Signature before trusting the payload.

schema.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature for verifying authenticity. Format: `t=<unix-seconds>,v1=<hex>`. The signed message is `{t}.{rawBody}` (the timestamp, a literal dot, and the raw JSON body), keyed by your webhook signing secret. Reject deliveries whose `t` is more than 300 seconds old to mitigate replay.

parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature for verifying authenticity. Format: `t=<unix-seconds>,v1=<hex>`. The signed message is `{t}.{rawBody}` (the timestamp, a literal dot, and the raw JSON body), keyed by your webhook signing secret. Reject deliveries whose `t` is more than 300 seconds old to mitigate replay.
parameters.name: X-Simmit-Signature

schema.description: Unique ID for the event. Stable across retries — use it to deduplicate deliveries.

parameters.description: Unique ID for the event. Stable across retries — use it to deduplicate deliveries.
parameters.name: X-Simmit-Event-Id

schema.description: Unique ID for this specific delivery attempt. Changes on each retry.

parameters.description: Unique ID for this specific delivery attempt. Changes on each retry.
parameters.name: X-Simmit-Delivery-Id

200.description: Return any 2xx status to acknowledge receipt. Non-2xx responses trigger retries.
